summary: Stellar second set with The Other One, Eyes, and tight China>Rider
blurb: The show shines on a monster second set featuring Truckin' into The Other One, Eyes of the World, and Morning Dew. Jerry’s vocals are in prime form and Keith delivers a flawless El Paso. A solid soundboard capture makes this performance a must‑listen.
review: February 28, 1973 at Salt Palace captured one of the Dead's classic early‑’73 outings. The first set rolls through bright folk‑rock numbers – Cold Rain And Snow, Sugaree and He’s Gone – with Jerry’s voice described as “in their prime” and Phil’s bass thundering like a “blow‑torching Jack Straw.”  The real fireworks erupt in the second set: a seamless China Cat Sunflower → I Know You Rider segue launches into a tight Truckin' that blossoms into an epic The Other One → Eyes Of The World → Morning Dew stretch, each jam praised for its melodic depth and rhythmic drive.  Row Jimmy shines with stellar harmonies, while Keith’s El Paso is called “pretty much perfect.”  Drum work from Bill Kreutzmann (and Mickey Hart on other nights) stays solid and supportive throughout the marathon.  Of the three available sources, the Jools‑captured SBD (gd1973-02-28.sbd.jools.9986.sbeok.shnf) offers the most complete picture despite a few hiss‑filled moments early on; its clarity from The Other One onward lets listeners fully appreciate the band’s locked‑in performance.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: All three recordings are marked positive, repeatedly praising the second‑set marathon (The Other One, Eyes, Morning Dew), tight segues, and standout moments like Jerry’s vocals and Keith’s El Paso. No negative commentary appears across sources, justifying a strong positive sentiment boost.

best_recording.reason: Good‑to‑great soundboard capture with clear audio from the second set onward, making it the most complete and listenable source despite early hiss.

key_highlights: Monster second set: Truckin' → The Other One → Eyes Of The World → Morning Dew
Seamless China Cat Sunflower > I Know You Rider segue sets a high‑energy tone
Jerry’s prime vocal performance and perfect Cold Rain & Snow solo
Keith’s flawless El Paso and overall balanced keyboard work

band_performance.Jerry: Vocals are described as in their prime and his lead guitar solo on Cold Rain & Snow is called perfect; strong lead vocal work throughout.
band_performance.Phil: Powerful, blow‑torching bass that drives the jams; intro to The Other One noted as loud/strong with bass‑drum bombs.
band_performance.Bob: Strong vocal contributions and harmonies, especially highlighted on Row Jimmy.
band_performance.Keith: El Paso is singled out as pretty much perfect; overall keyboard balance praised.
band_performance.Billy: Drum work is solid and supportive throughout the jams, providing a steady foundation.
